message:
test(daemon): add client auto-spawn, retry, and config E2E tests

Expand the daemon E2E test suite with two new test groups covering
the client-side connection management introduced in bd-2zll and
bd-35ft:

Client auto-spawn and retry tests (bd-2zll):
- test_no_auto_spawn_when_disabled: verifies that a ClientConfig with
  without_auto_spawn() fails immediately on health() without creating
  a socket file or spawning a daemon process
- test_connection_timeout_without_daemon: verifies that connection
  timeout is enforced within a reasonable bound when no daemon exists
- test_auto_spawn_creates_daemon: verifies that the default auto_spawn
  behavior starts a daemon process, creates the socket, and makes
  health() succeed
- test_retry_logic_connects_after_slow_start: simulates a daemon that
  takes time to start by spawning it with a delay, verifying the
  client's retry loop eventually connects
- test_multiple_clients_share_daemon: two clients connecting to the
  same socket path share one daemon instance (socket exists check)
- test_auto_spawn_respects_custom_socket_path: ClientConfig with a
  custom socket path spawns the daemon at that location
- test_client_reconnects_after_daemon_restart: stops and restarts a
  daemon, verifying the client re-establishes connection

Config file and environment variable tests (bd-35ft):
- test_config_file_sets_socket_path: writes a TOML config file and
  verifies ClientConfig::from_file() picks up the socket_path
- test_env_overrides_config_file: verifies XF_SOCKET_PATH env var
  takes precedence over the config file value
- test_default_config_without_file: verifies sensible defaults when
  no config file exists
- test_config_file_sets_idle_timeout: verifies idle_timeout_secs is
  read from config file
- test_env_overrides_idle_timeout: verifies XF_IDLE_TIMEOUT env var
  overrides config file idle_timeout_secs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
